text: Security is crucial in web development to protect both users and data. One basic step is using HTTPS, which encrypts information between the browser and server, preventing hackers from stealing sensitive details. Authentication systems, like passwords or two-factor login, ensure only authorised people can access accounts. However, even with these measures, common vulnerabilities remain. For example, input validation is essential because attackers can inject malicious code through forms or URLs, leading to data leaks. Developers must always check and clean user input. By understanding these threats and applying good practices, websites become much safer for everyone who uses them.
